title: Add & Remove Contacts
description: Extend the contact manager with add_contact() and remove_contact() functions. add_contact() takes name, phone, email and appends a new dict to the list. remove_contact() removes a contact by name (case-insensitive exact match) and returns True if found.
starter_code:
# Contact Manager — Step 3: Add & Remove
# Build on your previous code.

contacts = [
    {'name': 'Alice', 'phone': '555-0001', 'email': 'alice@example.com'},
    {'name': 'Bob', 'phone': '555-0002', 'email': 'bob@example.com'},
    {'name': 'Charlie', 'phone': '555-0003', 'email': 'charlie@example.com'},
]

def find_contact(contact_list, search_name):
    return [c for c in contact_list if search_name.lower() in c['name'].lower()]

def add_contact(contact_list, name, phone, email):
    """Add a new contact to the list."""
    # Your code here
    pass

def remove_contact(contact_list, name):
    """Remove a contact by exact name (case-insensitive). Returns True if found."""
    # Your code here
    return False

# Test it
add_contact(contacts, 'Diana', '555-0004', 'diana@example.com')
print(f"After add: {len(contacts)} contacts")
removed = remove_contact(contacts, 'bob')
print(f"Removed Bob: {removed}, now {len(contacts)} contacts")
test_code:
test_contacts = [
    {'name': 'Alice', 'phone': '555-0001', 'email': 'alice@example.com'},
]
add_contact(test_contacts, 'Eve', '555-0005', 'eve@example.com')
assert len(test_contacts) == 2, f'After add, should have 2 contacts, got {len(test_contacts)}'
assert test_contacts[1]['name'] == 'Eve', f"New contact name should be Eve"
assert test_contacts[1]['phone'] == '555-0005', 'Phone should match'
assert test_contacts[1]['email'] == 'eve@example.com', 'Email should match'
result = remove_contact(test_contacts, 'eve')
assert result is True, 'remove_contact should return True when found'
assert len(test_contacts) == 1, f'After remove, should have 1 contact, got {len(test_contacts)}'
result2 = remove_contact(test_contacts, 'nobody')
assert result2 is False, 'remove_contact should return False when not found'
acceptance_criteria: add_contact() creates a new dict and appends to list
remove_contact() removes by exact name match (case-insensitive)
remove_contact() returns True if found, False otherwise
hints: add_contact: create a dict and use .append()
remove_contact: loop through the list, compare names with .lower()
To remove from a list: contact_list.remove(item) or use a filtered list
